behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git rename detection plus CLI parsing/reporting. A mock status/diff stream can hide real subprocess output shapes and rename heuristics, so real repo + real git invocation is needed. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is only git/change categorization and stdout reporting, with no AI/ML decision step, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI against that repo, and assert report has the file only in `renamed` and not in `added`/`deleted`.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: This failure commonly appears at real subprocess/encoding boundaries (binary patch output, byte decoding, git diff behavior). In-process fakes often miss the exact binary-output edge cases. Rejected higher levels: no model output quality or multi-agent pipeline in behavior text, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, add/modify a binary file (e.g., random bytes), run the CLI, assert process exits successfully and report includes binary file changes in counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Unborn-HEAD behavior is defined by real git command exit codes/messages in a real repository state. Mocked git calls can hide this boundary and always return happy-path data. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is git/CLI error handling only, not AI/ML output-dependent, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with no commits, run the CLI, assert zero exit failure and an empty structured report (no categories populated).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This validates end-to-end correctness against true git history semantics across commits/renames/deletes/modifies. Real repository state and real git history traversal are the failure boundary; stubs can miss drift from actual git behavior. Rejected higher levels: behavior text contains no AI/ML call or multi-agent sequence, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a real repo with a known sequence of commits covering add/delete/rename/modify, run the CLI over that history window, and assert each category count equals ground truth derived from git history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged semantics depend on real git index and working-tree behavior. In-process mocks typically flatten these states and can hide failures in command selection/flag usage. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is git state comparison only, with no AI/ML dependency, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create equivalent staged and unstaged change scenarios, run CLI in each mode/path, and assert normalized structured output is identical.
